
# Octopath Traveler 0 + CotC Event Opcode Crossref Atlas

This document focuses only on event-command opcodes. It resolves event payload IDs into concrete database assets and also pulls in broader context from the surrounding database: owning `EventList` rows, map names, nested event targets, local target-handle aliases, battle-event labels, text rows, sounds, effects, textures, curves, and the native `AEventManager` wrapper table.

## Field Resolver Map

| Field | Resolves To | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `m_Text` | `GameText/TalkText/GameTextEvent.m_id` / `GameText/Localize/[LANG CODE]/TalkText/GameTextEvent.m_id` | Event dialogue line IDs resolve cleanly to (English) talk text. |
| `m_EventId` | `Event/EventList.m_id` | This resolves to the EventList row, then to the real event asset via `m_Name` and `m_Version`. |
| `m_NarrationId` | `Narration/NarrationList.m_id` | NarrationList rows carry `m_TextLavel` IDs which in turn resolve back into GameTextEvent. |
| `m_SoundId` | `Sound/SoundList.m_id` | Used by SE/voice/BGM-family event commands. |
| `m_ItemId` | `Item/ItemList.m_id` | `ItemList.m_NameID` then resolves to GameTextItem. |
| `m_MapId` | `Map/MapListTable.m_id` | MapListTable gives the map label/path and m_MapText which resolves to GameTextMap. |
| `m_ObjectId` | `Object/ObjectList.m_id` | ObjectList gives the in-level object label used by numeric object commands. |
| `m_CharaDataId` | `Character/CharaID.m_id` | Useful for spawn/join opcodes: gives the sprite sheet / battle texture record. |
| `m_CharaAnimId` | `Character/CharacterAnimationID.m_id` | Resolves to animation table rows with loop/type/name fields. |
| `m_EffectId` | `Effect/EffectList.m_id` | EffectList carries the effect label and particle asset path. |
| `m_TextureId` | `Texture/TextureID.m_id` | Event texture swaps overwhelmingly point into TextureID, not CharaTexID/EnemyTexID. |
| `m_CurveID` | `Trigger/CurveID.m_id` | Curve-based movement opcodes resolve to named curve assets here. |

## IDA Switch Coverage Overview

- OT0 opcode rows observed in the DB: `225` unique opcodes.
- Opcode entries with direct interpreter switch evidence: `178`.
- Opcode entries that still lack a semantic meaning note: `165`.
- The unresolved summary below is intentionally kept even after adding IDA case bullets because many opcodes have a confirmed callee path but still need stronger semantic naming.

## Switch-Confirmed Variant Families

| Family Root | Related Opcodes | Switch/Handler Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `23` | `23`, `24` | `sub_144621910` movement family with mode split `0/1` |
| `41` | `41`, `43` | `sub_144631AC0` family with mode split `0/1` |
| `200` | `200`, `202`, `205` | `sub_14462E810` family with shared setup and mode variants |
| `277` | `277`, `278` | `sub_144621BD0` curve-movement family with mode split `0/1` |
| `700` | `700`, `703` | `sub_14462F020` effect family with mode split `0/1` |
| `921` | `921`, `922` | `sub_14462E3D0` global-manager family with mode split `0/1` |
| `5010` | `5010`, `5012` | `sub_14461F930` family with mode split `0/1` |
| `5502` | `5502`, `5503` | `sub_1446276D0` family with mode split `1/0` |
| `21031` | `21031`, `21032` | `sub_14464A6C0` family where 21032 performs extra setup first |
